description: Fix stale fetchJSON code examples in guide pages that use the unchecked-cast overload without handling the `| undefined` return type, causing TypeScript strict mode errors if copied verbatim.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSON code example in streaming-progress guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the streaming-progress guide's code example to compile under TypeScript strict mode so that I can copy it verbatim without type errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The `export_messages` tool example in streaming-progress.mdx handles the `| undefined` return type from `fetchJSON<T>()` — either by adding a null guard (e.g., `if (!result) throw ToolError.internal(...)`) or by switching to the Zod schema overload which returns `T` (not `T | undefined`)
The code example remains clear and pedagogical — the fix should not obscure the progress-reporting pattern being demonstrated
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx (lines 46-54). The issue is on line 50-53: `const result = await fetchJSON<{...}>(url)` returns `T | undefined` (the unchecked-cast overload from platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts line 136). Line 52 then does `messages.push(...result.messages)` and line 53 does `cursor = result.nextCursor` — both fail under strict null checks because `result` could be `undefined`. The simplest fix is to add a null guard after the fetchJSON call: `if (!result) throw ToolError.internal('Unexpected empty response');`. This also requires adding `ToolError` to the import on line 20. Also update the `lastUpdated` frontmatter from 2026-02-21 to 2026-02-28.
